title: Modern Rustic X Console Table
summary: A slatted-shelf console with X-braced ends, built almost entirely from 2x2 with a pocket-hole jig. Optionally paired into a couch-spanning bridge.
description:
A behind-the-couch console with a lower slatted shelf and decorative X braces on the ends — a clean modern-rustic look from cheap 2x2 stock. The whole frame is pocket-holed together, and the lower shelf is a run of 1x3 slats spaced with an even gap so it reads as a deliberate slat pattern rather than a solid shelf.

The materials here build one console; the original was built as a pair with a plywood bridge spanning between them behind a sofa, so if that's your goal, double the list and add bridge parts. On its own it's a single, tidy console.

The slat spacing is the detail that sells it. Attach the center 1x3 slat first, then work outward keeping a consistent 7/8" gap — starting from the center means any leftover fraction lands evenly at both ends instead of leaving one lopsided gap at one side. The X pieces are cut to fit rather than to a number: mark them in place against the assembled end, cut, and fit.

tools.note: The entire frame and shelf are pocket-holed.

materials.name: 2x2, 8 ft
materials.note: Legs, ends, shelf trim, X pieces.

materials.name: 1x12, 3 ft
materials.note: Top. A 3/4" plywood piece 11-1/4" x 31-1/4" also works.

cutList.note: Cut to fit the end openings.

steps.title: Build the end assemblies
steps.body: Set the Kreg jig to the 1-1/2" setting. Drill a single pocket hole in each end of the 11-1/4" 2x2 end rails and attach them to the legs with 2-1/2" pocket screws, centering the middle rail on the legs. This forms the two ends of the console.

steps.title: Connect the ends
steps.body: Drill a single 1-1/2" pocket hole in each end of the 31-1/4" 2x2 front/back rails and attach them to the legs with 2-1/2" pocket screws, tying the two ends into a table frame.

steps.title: Add the slatted shelf
steps.body: Reset the jig to the 3/4" setting and drill two pocket holes in each end of every 1x3 slat. Attach one slat centered in the middle of the shelf frame first, then work outward with a consistent 7/8" gap. Starting from the center keeps the leftover gap even at both ends instead of leaving one odd gap on one side.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Drill 3/4" pocket holes every 6–8" along the edges of the 1x12 or plywood top and attach it to the inside top of the frame. A brad-nailer-and-cleats method also works if you'd rather not see pocket holes underneath.

steps.title: Fit the X braces
steps.body: Cut the smaller X pieces to fit the end openings — mark them in place against the assembled end, cut, and fit rather than working to a number. Fasten them in, sand, and finish. If you're building the couch-bridge version, repeat everything for a second console and span a 1x12 and 2x2s between the two.

images.alt: Modern rustic console table with X-braced ends and a slatted lower shelf
